problem_statement: Nested Mind exposes a distributed lease boundary that converts scheduler jobs into backend-specific lease claim requests and verifies claim receipts against job id, worker id, payload hash, lease expiry, and optional fencing token. Mullu Control Plane already has temporal lease-window receipts that prove runtime ownership after a lease exists, but it lacks a prior distributed lease claim receipt boundary that binds the scheduler job identity, worker identity, backend kind, request payload hash, operation payload hash, plan hash, approval refs, adapter claim receipt refs, 2xx response status, response hash, observed payload hash, lease expiry, fencing token, and no-local-backend-call flags before any distributed scheduler lease claim can be admitted.
success_criteria: Distributed lease claim plans build deterministic backend operation payloads for supported lease backends and bind request payload hash, operation payload hash, policy hash, and plan hash
Plan-only mode emits an explicit non-live receipt that does not claim lease acquisition, perform backend calls, authenticate requests, or store secrets
Dry-run mode rejects approval refs, adapter claim receipt refs, response status, response hash, observed payload hash, lease expiry, and fencing token evidence

Claim-approved mode requires operator approval, adapter claim receipt ref, 2xx response status, response hash, observed payload hash matching the expected job payload hash, future lease expiry, and a fencing token when policy requires fencing before external claim admission
Observed payload hash mismatch, expired lease grants, missing fencing tokens, oversized lease windows, missing evidence refs, and disabled receipt policy all block admission with explicit reasons
Raw token, JWT, or private-key shaped material in metadata or refs blocks receipt admission
The distributed lease claim receipt is schema-backed, indexed in the governance protocol manifest, and included in proof coverage matrix witnesses
No live Postgres, Redis, etcd, Consul, SQLite, HTTP gateway, scheduler mutation, worker dispatch, deployment, or production readiness claim is introduced
non_goals: no live distributed lease backend call
constraints: claim-approved receipts must bind external adapter evidence instead of executing the distributed lease claim in the planner
plan-only and dry-run modes must remain free of external effects and live claim evidence
expected and observed payload hashes must use sha256-prefixed hex digests and must match before claim admission
claim-approved receipts must require a future lease expiry and fencing token when policy requires fencing
lease duration must be positive and must not exceed policy max_lease_seconds
raw token, JWT, or private-key shaped material must fail closed
receipt generation must be deterministic and schema-valid
evidence_required: focused distributed lease claim test receipt
standalone SDLC requirement and design validation receipt
workspace governance preflight receipt before closure claim
